justification: All 4 business_impact sub-fields (financial, operational, user, risk) are populated with substantive, multi-sentence analysis. Stakeholders_affected lists 7 distinct groups (B2C recipients, B2B customers, drivers, dispatch/ops, customer support, sales/account management, product/competitive strategy) each with specific impact descriptions. Multiple escalations for missing information are present in the verdict section (B2B contract review, service tier scoping, display-only vs systemic). Contractual SLA risk is explicitly and prominently identified. Confidence level is 78, well within the <=80 threshold, with detailed assumptions listed. Competitive positioning is thoroughly addressed in financial, user, and risk sections, plus a dedicated stakeholder entry for product/competitive strategy.

observations.text: Exceptional stakeholder granularity — 7 distinct groups identified vs. the 4 minimum required, each with specific and differentiated impact analysis
Contractual SLA risk is identified prominently in both the risk section and as a rejection reason, with clear escalation to Legal
Strong escalation in the verdict — 4 explicit questions that must be answered before proceeding, including the critical B2B contract review
Competitive positioning thoroughly analyzed across multiple sections, noting the counter-trend nature of the change
Confidence level of 78 is well-calibrated, with clearly articulated high/low confidence areas and explicit assumptions
Excellent edge cases identified — cross-timezone ambiguity, same-day/express carve-outs, perishable goods, end-of-day definition
Financial analysis captures both sides — SLA penalty reduction vs. premium tier revenue risk and competitive churn
Operational analysis balances the positive (reduced routing pressure) against the negative (potential WISMO increase)

agent_output_excerpt:
# Domain Analysis — Delivery ETA Display Change

## From Exact Timestamps → Date-Only Windows

---

```yaml
domain_analysis:
  business_context: >
    Delivery ETA communication is a core touchpoint in the courier/logistics domain.
    The proposal changes the granularity of the delivery promise from an exact timestamp
    ("Delivery by 14:30 on Friday") to a date-only window ("Delivery by Friday").
    This affects the fundamental contract between the logistics provider and the recipient —
